summary: Replacement systems_architect performed a fresh read-only review of current HEAD f7e203feb48a30e8606019640391273c19f252dd and writer commit 15a337f3b70e947adc4fbe7c592d49f7702207be. Mechanical tests pass and changed paths stay within the task's allowed package/test/project-message scope, but M6 diplomacy/legitimacy commands currently accept unauthorized actors and the recognized-order obligation reason branch is dead code, so the task is not architecturally acceptable yet.

tests_run.result: On feat/m6-diplomacy-legitimacy at f7e203fe; only pre-existing untracked ui-samples/ is present.
Writer implementation changes are in allowed package/test/project-message/task paths: sim-core, sim-ai, protocol, save-format, tests, and project metadata/messages.
Post-writer changes are process routing/replacement metadata only under project paths.
No selected package has a typecheck script; this is package-script absence, not a TypeScript diagnostic.
Node/worker-compatible hashes matched for hello, command/query, save/load, M4 replay, and M5 playable loop canaries.
Architecture boundaries passed; determinism passed; root Vitest 37 files / 257 tests passed; web Vitest 1 file / 4 tests passed.

tests_run.command: rg literal boundary/determinism scans over reviewed source
tests_run.result: No React/Pixi/Electron/Node IO imports, Math.random, Date.now, performance.now, eval, new Function, dynamic import, any, unsafe double assertion, TODO/FIXME, skip, or .only found in reviewed source/test files; dynamic import hit only existing sim-core performance-baseline script outside authority source.
sim.propose-diplomatic-agreement, sim.answer-diplomatic-agreement, and sim.record-legitimacy-source all returned accepted when actor was {kind:'player', id:'polity:999'} against polity 1/2 state.

decisions: REQUEST_CHANGES: do not accept M6-DIPLOMACY-LEGITIMACY-001 until command authority and recognized-order obligation semantics are fixed and covered by tests.
No forbidden path modifications were found in the writer diff or current HEAD process diff; PROJECT_STATUS.md, UI/desktop/content/tools/docs gate/roadmap/acceptance matrix paths were not modified.
No manual node battle, server/multiplayer, telemetry, arbitrary-code scripting, full peace treaty UI, hidden grand-strategy manager, or implicit RPC surface was found in the reviewed implementation.

M6 state is represented in sim-core with branded IDs, schemaVersion 1, canonical ordering, reason-coded domain events/deltas, save DTOs, and save parser rejection for malformed/missing/future-version M6 payloads when the runtime expects M6 state.
Protocol/save changes appear additive/versioned and M1-M5 determinism tests still pass.
risks: Mechanical tests pass but currently miss unauthorized diplomacy/legitimacy mutation paths.

M6 AI currently constructs commands with actor id ai:m6 in tests, unlike existing campaign AI conventions that use actor kind ai with id polity:<id>; adding authority checks will require aligning AI/test actor IDs or defining a narrow M6 authority rule.

The current recognized-order obligation branch cannot emit obligation-risk because activeObligationCount >= 0 is always true; this weakens the acceptance claim that obligations affect start-to-victory decisions.
Untracked ui-samples/ remains in the worktree and was not inspected or modified.

blockers.issue: M6 diplomacy/legitimacy commands do not enforce actor authority. Any player/AI actor can propose between arbitrary polities, answer agreements, or record legitimacy sources for any polity. This violates the existing command validation model used by M3/M4 actorHasPolityAuthority and makes legitimacy/victory pressure directly forgeable through the public GameCommand union.
blockers.required_fix: Add authority validation and tests: proposer must be authorized for proposerPolityId, answer must be authorized for the responding polity or explicitly system-scoped, and record-legitimacy-source must be restricted to system/domain-authorized paths or a documented authority rule. Update M6 AI to emit actor IDs compatible with that authority convention, and add rejection tests proving unauthorized player/AI actors cannot mutate M6 state.

blockers.issue: Recognized-order obligation reasoning has a dead branch: activeObligationCount >= 0 is always true, so m6.recognized-order.obligation-risk cannot be produced and obligations do not affect canPursueVictory. This does not satisfy the task requirement that legitimacy/recognized-order decisions account for obligations.
blockers.required_fix: Define the intended obligation semantics for M6 recognized-order decisions, implement a non-tautological rule, and add tests covering both obligation-clear and obligation-risk/blocked cases. If obligations are only informational, update the task acceptance claim before review; otherwise make canPursueVictory or reason-coded pressure reflect active/overdue obligation state.

requested_action: Route REQUEST_CHANGES to the replacement simulation_engineer. After fixes, return for another systems_architect review with focused tests for M6 actor authority, AI actor ID parity, and obligation-sensitive recognized-order decisions.
